text: my neighbor mows his lawn at 7am on saturdays and i have considered arson
someone at work keeps setting the thermostat to 64 and i will find you
the walmart greeter at 2am just stared through me like i wasnt real
bought a vcr at a yard sale for $3 and it had a blockbuster tape still in it
my packages keep disappearing and i know its the guy in 4B
the speed bump on elm street literally launched my coffee into the windshield
toll booth guy said have a nice day and i almost started crying
i judge everyone who buys more than 3 frozen pizzas at once. im everyone
my windshield wiper fluid is empty and i am choosing to live like this
my umbrella broke in the parking lot and nobody helped. noted
heard my upstairs neighbor vacuuming at midnight again. what are you hiding
the self checkout judged me today. i could feel it
saw my coworker microwave fish again. we are not okay
someone put a passive aggressive note on my car about parking. its my driveway
i cry in my car during lunch and call it self care
walmart at 2am is the only honest place left in america
the toll booth is the only place anyone asks how i am anymore
bought a painting at a yard sale and found a $20 taped to the back
if you slam your door one more time 6C im calling the landlord and god
my neighbor has 14 garden gnomes and they all face my window
office thermostat is at 72 and someone is wearing a blanket. pick a side
the speed bump didnt slow me down it radicalized me
someone returned a half eaten rotisserie chicken at costco today. respect
i keep buying stuff at yard sales i dont need because the old lady was nice
my package was marked delivered and i have trust issues now
there is a man in walmart right now wearing a bathrobe and crocs. king
the apartment above me is either bowling or moving furniture every single night
whoever keeps stealing my newspaper im not even subscribed anymore its ads
i told the toll booth guy about my divorce and he just nodded. enough
my wiper fluid froze and i am driving by faith alone
the lady in front of me at aldi had 47 coupons. i counted
someone in the office kitchen labeled their milk with a threat. fair
my umbrella turned inside out and a stranger laughed. i hope ur next
yard sale guy tried to sell me his childhood trauma for $5
the thermostat has a lock on it now. this is tyranny
walmart pharmacy line at 2am is where hope goes to die
my neighbor pressure washes his driveway every week. what did it do to you
the speed bumps on my street have speed bumps. its personal now
i eat lunch in my car because the breakroom smells like betrayal
package said out for delivery 6 hours ago. the lies never stop
toll booth at 5am hits different when you havent slept
my upstairs neighbor has a treadmill and uses it at 11pm. why
bought a lamp at a yard sale that doesnt work. i am the lamp
someone at work brought in a space heater and blew the fuse. hero
the walmart parking lot at 3am is a lawless wasteland and i love it
my coworker clips his nails at his desk. i have seen everything
i left my umbrella on the bus and it started raining immediately. of course
neighbor put up a ring camera pointed directly at my porch. cool cool cool
the grocery store moved the bread aisle and i almost didnt make it
wiper fluid light has been on for 3 weeks. we're past that now
someone in my building plays trumpet at 9pm. not well
i go through the toll booth slowly so the guy has to talk to me longer
the speed bump on oak ave is a hate crime against honda civics
my neighbors kids keep kicking a ball into my yard. its mine now
i bought a mystery box at a yard sale. it was full of vhs tapes of jeopardy
whoever is microwaving popcorn at 8am please explain yourself
walmart self checkout asked if i need help and i almost said yes emotionally
the thermostat says 70 but my soul says 58
my package was delivered to the wrong house and they opened it. animals
i saw a raccoon in the walmart parking lot and we understood each other
lunch in the car again. at least the radio doesnt judge me
the apartment dryer has had the same sock in it for 3 months. whose is it
my neighbor leaf blows his leaves into my yard. war was declared
someone at the grocery store said nice cart and i think about it daily
umbrella broke on the way to a job interview. the universe has notes
i found a $50 jacket at a yard sale with $20 in the pocket. profit
the toll booth lane with no attendant feels like rejection
my upstairs neighbor dropped something heavy at 3am and didnt even check on me
the office fridge is a crime scene and nobody is investigating
speed bump took out my muffler. i will be sending an invoice to the city
wiper fluid is a luxury in this economy tbh
walmart at 1am is just insomniacs and philosophers with shopping carts
someone in my complex has wind chimes. i have a plan
i cried at a yard sale because the old man said i reminded him of his son
the person who adjusts the thermostat after me is my nemesis
my porch camera caught the delivery guy yeet my package over the fence
grocery store put the good cheese behind a lock. we live in a society
i eat lunch alone and pretend im on a very important phone call
the neighbors are having a party on a tuesday. some of us have regrets to sleep on
my umbrella collection is just a graveyard of failed promises
toll booth therapy is cheaper than real therapy and equally effective
there are 4 speed bumps on my street. thats one per grudge the city has against us
the walmart jewelry counter at 2am is surprisingly busy. love is alive
someone stole my doormat. what kind of person steals a doormat
found a working gameboy at a yard sale. i am reborn
my apartment walls are so thin i know my neighbors wifi password from hearing them say it
the office thermostat is controlled by someone who has never been cold in their life
wiper fluid nozzle is clogged and i just accept the blur now
the laundry room has a sign that says be kind. ironic given the sock thief
my coworker eats hard boiled eggs at her desk. daily. i am suffering
neighbor complained about my dog barking. sir your rooster wakes me at 5am
walmart greeter remembered my name and now i have to switch locations
i bought an entire set of encyclopedias at a yard sale for $1. knowledge is cheap
the grocery store sample lady gave me two samples. i am chosen
someone on my street has a leaf blower at 6am. violence is never the answer but
i forgot my umbrella and it rained. i remembered it and it was sunny. cursed
the speed bump near my house has a pothole after it. combo attack
lunch break is 30 min and i spend 20 of it staring at the steering wheel
my neighbor waters his lawn in the rain. i have questions
the toll booth guy waved at me today and i felt seen for the first time in weeks
apartment elevator smells different every day and i dont want to know why
someone took my lunch from the work fridge. it had my name on it. justice is dead
